course_description:
This course has a number of objectives; to outline the problem of climate change, to discuss and compare the theoretical economic solutions to combating climate change, to present existing climate change mitigation actions in an economic context and to discuss possible future climate policy issues.

In this course students will learn to analyse climate change using economic approaches and concepts. By the end of the course students should not only have a greater awareness of the role of climate policy, but also be able to analyse and critically evaluate policy issues using basic microeconomic tools. Topics covered include economics of stabilisation, economics instruments, clean development mechanism, international agreements, adaptation, and Australia climate policy.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:

course_aims: The purpose of ECON7760 is to provide students with an economic framework that may be used to analyse climate change issues.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate knowledge of the causes (and potential regulatory solutions) of climate change.
Understand and apply economic concepts to the issue of climate change.
Critically evaluate the economic outcome of climate change policy.
Communicate economic thinking in non-technical, plain language via different formats.

assessment_summary.title: Quizzes
assessment_summary.due_date: In-Tutorial Week 5,; In-Tutorial Week 7,; In-Tutorial Week 11,; Students will only be able to complete the quiz in their enrolled tutorial.

assessment_details.title: Quizzes
assessment_details.due_date: In-Tutorial Week 5, In-Tutorial Week 7, In-Tutorial Week 11, Students will only be able to complete the quiz in their enrolled tutorial.
assessment_details.task_description: Student IDs are mandatory. Students must present a valid university-issued ID card to be admitted into the room for the quiz. Without it, entry will not be permitted. Students are required to complete three in-class quizzes covering the materials Students will have 30 minutes to complete each quiz. There will be true/false, multiple choice, and numerical questions in this quiz. Students should be at the venue for your quiz on time in the weeks of assessment. You must attend your allocated tutorial. If you arrive late to the session, you will not receive additional time to complete the quiz. The quizzes allow one (1) typed double sided A4 Page Cheat Sheet with standard margins, 1.5 line spacing minimum, and 12pt Font Size (Times New Roman or Calibri Fonts Required). All notes MUST be in English. Cheat Sheets must be handed in with your quiz at time for submission. This will be checked and non-compliance with these requirements accounts for academic misconduct. Only UQ approved calculators are allowed. More details regarding the format of the quiz will be posted to Blackboard under Assessment. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submitted in tutorial.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 3 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Applications for a quiz extension can be submitted no later than 3 calendar days after the date of the original in-person quiz. Late submissions for quiz extensions will not be accepted.
Note: If applying for an extension due to medical reasons, your medical certificate must cover the date of the in-class quiz based on your enrolled tutorial.
Extensions are a one-time opportunity only for this assessment. The date and time for the replacement in-tutorial quiz are set by the course coordinator within 2-weeks of the original date. Students must make themselves available to sit the quiz at the scheduled extension time. There are no extensions on extensions for this assessment.
Late submission
You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late .

assessment_details.title: Infographic and Presentation
assessment_details.task_description: Students will be required to design an infographic on an issue relating to the first 7 weeks of course content on climate economics. Students must also record a 3–4-minute presentation on the infographic explaining the purpose and analysis provided by the infographic. It is required that students are physically and clearly visible in the recording. We recommend using Zoom or Teams to record your infographic presentation. Please See Blackboard for specific question, details, and criteria. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A f ailure to reference generative AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit via Turnitin, Blackboard by the due date/time specified.
Submissions via email will not be accepted. All assessment MUST be submitted via Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Extensions are restricted to 14 days to ensure that students receive timely feedback before the subsequent assessment item, due to the structured progression of the assessment in this course.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Climate Policy Brief (maximum 1 page)
assessment_details.task_description: As part of the policy brief, students are expected to conduct through research on the selected policy issue, analyse the economic aspects of the policy issue and use economic graphs to illustrate key relationships, trends or comparisons. Page Limit: 1 page (single-sided). A maximum two graphs, tables, or other illustrations in total can be provided on a second page. Margin Size: Minimum of a 1.5cm margins on all sides. Smaller margins will result in a penalty. Font Size: Use a legible font size 11-point such as Times New Roman, Calibri or Arial. Line Spacing: Minimum single line spacing Please See Blackboard for specific question, details, and criteria. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.
